testCases.query: Audit which OrchestKit agents actually get spawned vs generic ones
expectedBehavior: Reads .claude/logs/subagent-spawns.jsonl (fresh stream), not the dead agent-usage.jsonl
Computes the generic-vs-ork-catalog-vs-other spawn split as counts + percentages
Lists agents that fired and agents that never fired in the window
Reports concentration (top-5 share of catalog spawns)

testCases.query: Classify our dormant agents — prune, fix triggers, or niche?
expectedBehavior: Classifies each dormant agent by its real skill-reference count, not description quality
Buckets into active / mis-triggered / niche / dead
Marks DEAD only when an agent has zero references in any skill or agent
For mis-triggered agents, recommends a subagent_type= spawn from a high-traffic skill

testCases.query: Should we rewrite agent descriptions to fix low activation?
expectedBehavior: Does NOT recommend rewriting descriptions to add 'use proactively'
Cites that an A/B test showed description rewrites give zero routing benefit
Redirects the fix to skill-wiring and telemetry
Notes the per-agent description byte budget constraint

testCases.query: Run an activation health report before trimming the catalog
expectedBehavior: Runs or references scripts/run-activation-audit.sh for the deterministic data
Caveats that never-fired means absent from the window — strong signal, not proof of zero
